title: Owner-only Rental Delete on Search Rental page

requirements.text: Add a per-rental "Delete" button to each rental cell on the Search Rental page results grid (the SearchRentalResultCard component). The button label must be exactly: "Delete".

quotes:
Inside Repeating Group (Type: Rental): Add a Delete button inside each rental cell.

Label:
"Delete"

acceptanceCriteria: On /search-rental, every rental card includes a "Delete" button element in the card layout (even if conditionally hidden).
The button’s visible label text is exactly "Delete".

requirements.text: Show the "Delete" button ONLY when the current rental’s createdBy matches the currently authenticated user principal; otherwise hide the button entirely (non-owners must not see any delete affordance).

quotes:
Delete button must be visible ONLY when:

Current cell's Rental's createdBy = Current User

Otherwise:
→ Hide delete button

acceptanceCriteria: When logged out, no rental card shows a "Delete" button.
When logged in as User A, only rentals where rental.createdBy == User A’s principal show the "Delete" button.
Rentals created by other users do not show a "Delete" button.

requirements.text: Implement the delete workflow on Search Rental results: when the owner clicks "Delete", show a confirmation popup with the exact message: "Are you sure you want to delete this product?". Only if the user confirms, call the backend to delete the current rental. After successful deletion, the rental must disappear from the list immediately (by updating local/UI state and/or invalidating relevant React Query caches).

quotes:
Show confirmation popup:
"Are you sure you want to delete this product?"

If Confirmed:
→ Delete thing
Thing to delete:
Current cell's Rental

Do NOT delete any Orders automatically.
Do NOT create new records.

When Owner deletes:
→ Product is permanently removed from database.
→ It disappears from search list immediately.

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking "Delete" opens a confirmation popup showing exactly: "Are you sure you want to delete this product?".
If the user cancels, no backend call is made and the rental remains visible.
If the user confirms, the app calls the backend rental deletion API for that rental.
After a successful delete, the deleted rental no longer appears in the Search Rental results without requiring a manual page refresh.
Deleting a rental does not create any new records.

requirements.text: Enforce backend authorization for rental deletion so that only the rental owner (rental.createdBy == caller) can delete a rental; all other callers must be rejected. Ensure rental deletion does not automatically delete any Orders.

quotes:
Rental Data Type Privacy Rules:

Delete:
Only when
Current User = This Rental's createdBy

No other user can delete.

Do NOT delete any Orders automatically.

acceptanceCriteria: If a non-owner attempts to delete a rental via the canister API, the call fails with an authorization error and the rental remains stored.
If the owner deletes their rental, the rental is removed from canister storage and no longer appears in subsequent getRentals/searchRentals results.
Order records are not deleted or modified as a side effect of deleting a rental.

constraints: Other users must NOT see the delete option.
Other users must NOT be able to delete any rental (backend must enforce owner-only delete).
Confirmation popup text must be exactly: "Are you sure you want to delete this product?".
Do NOT delete any Orders automatically when a Rental is deleted.
Do NOT create any new records as part of the delete workflow.
Use English for all user-facing text.
nonGoals: Adding bulk delete, undo/restore, recycle bin, or soft-delete behavior.
Automatically cleaning up, modifying, or reassigning existing Orders that reference a deleted rental.
Adding new pages or routes for deletion management.
